facts.text: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <feed xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xml:lang="en-US"> <title>Tantek Çelik</title> <link href="https://tantek.com/" rel="alternate" title="Tantek Çelik" type="text/html"/> <link href="https://tantek.com/updates.atom" rel="self"/> <link rel="hub" href="https://pubsubhubbub.superfeedr.com/"/> <id>https://tantek.com/updates.atom</id> <author> <name>Tantek</name> <uri>https://tantek.com/</uri> </author> <updated>2026-02-24T23:45:00-08:00</updated> <entry xmlns:default="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" xmlns:default1="http://activitystrea.ms/spec/1.0/"> <updated>2026-02-24T23:45:00-08:00</updated> <published>2026-02-24T23:45:00-08:00</published> <link href="https://tantek.com/2026/055/t2/solution-choice-firefox-ai-controls" rel="alternate" title="" type="text/html"/> <id>https://tantek.com/2026/055/t2/solution-choice-firefox-ai-controls</id> <title type="xhtml"> <default:div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" class="if-your-feed-reader-displays-this-then-it-is-violating-the-Atom-spec-RFC-4287-section-4.2.14"/> </title> <content type="xhtml" xml:base="https://tantek.com/" xml:space="preserve"> <default:div xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" xml:space="preserve">“Choice.

The solution is choice.”*<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>You should download Firefox 148 (released today!) and explicitly set the new "AI Controls" to your preferred choice.<default:br class="auto-break"/>* <default:a class="auto-link" href="https://www.firefox.com/">https://www.firefox.com/</default:a><default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>Disclosure: I work for Mozilla, but this post, like all on this site, represents my personal thoughts and opinions.<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>More and more software includes various “AI” features.

The “quotes” are deliberate because there is an increasingly fuzzy popular understanding of what is or is not “AI” that continues to diverge from any specific technical meaning.<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>Many folks have expressed strong opinions against “AI” features (for lots of reasons which are worth a separate blog post), in particular in web browsers, and a desire for a simple way to disable such features.<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>Tentatively called an “AI kill switch”, the Firefox team developed both an overall switch to turn off or block various "AI" features by default (including any future features), and the ability to selectively enable specific features.

Or vice versa (turn on by default, and selectively disable specific features).<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>See the official blog post for screenshots and lots more details:<default:br class="auto-break"/>* <default:a class="auto-link" href="https://blog.mozilla.org/en/firefox/how-to-use-ai-controls/">https://blog.mozilla.org/en/firefox/how-to-use-ai-controls/</default:a><default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>I have set my own "Block AI enhancements" setting to "blocked", with the exception of enabling "Translations".

rules.text: - The solution is choice.”*<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>You should download Firefox 148 (released today!) and explicitly set the new "AI Controls" to your preferred choice.<default:br class="auto-break"/>* <default:a class="auto-link" href="https://www.firefox.com/">https://www.firefox.com/</default:a><default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>Disclosure: I work for Mozilla, but this post, like all on this site, represents my personal thoughts and opinions.<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>More and more software includes various “AI” features.

The solution is choice.”*<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>You should download Firefox 148 (released today!) and explicitly set the new "AI Controls" to your preferred choice.<default:br class="auto-break"/>* <default:a class="auto-link" href="https://www.firefox.com/">https://www.firefox.com/</default:a><default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>Disclosure: I work for Mozilla, but this post, like all on this site, represents my personal thoughts and opinions.<default:br class="auto-break"/><default:br class="auto-break"/>More and more software includes various “AI” features.
